text:
Once upon a time in the bright city of Metropolis lived a clumsy writer named Freddy Moonbeam. Freddy wanted to write about adventures, but his plots always ended badly. He dreamed of mice, but his mice were monsters. His rabbits? Suicide bombers. So he switched subjects.

He read about moths. Moths good. Moths good for stories.

So Freddy scribbled till dawn. He dreamed about silent nocturnal flyers weaving through city mist. He sketched beautiful sketches.

Then one moonlit night, Freddy noticed something weird. On his windowsill.

An inchworm.

An inchworm? Freddy stared. He grabbed his crayons.

One day an inchworm crawled onto his laptop screen.

Freddy drew it a spiral staircase.

He wrote about an inchworm who became a hero. Saved skyscrapers.

Nope. Still monsters inside me.

Maybe moths.

He drew them black. Deadly.

He wrote about them flying into buildings.

Yeah. That sounded moog
